turns.thought: [OBSERVE] The swarm is completely stalled: diversity ~9.7e-15 (normalized ~2.6e-18), velocity zero ratio 1.0, all particles have identical fitness (best, mean, worst all 0.10084), fitness std ~4.2e-17. No improvement for 200 iterations. The swarm has collapsed to a single point. | [DIAGNOSE] Premature convergence to a local minimum of the Griewank function. Diversity has entirely collapsed, and all particles are trapped in a single basin with zero velocity. Without external perturbation, the swarm will remain frozen indefinitely. | [STRATEGIZE] We must inject fresh diversity to break out of this local basin. Randomly reinitializing a significant fraction of particles (30%) with new positions and velocities will spread candidates across the search space, potentially near better basins. An inertia weight >1 (1.1) will encourage continued exploration after reset.

turns.thought: [REFLECT] The reset of 30% of particles successfully injected diversity into the swarm, but the global best remained unchanged at 0.1008 after the immediate reset. This indicates that the randomly reinitialized particles did not luckily land in a superior basin. However, they now have non-zero velocities and are exploring the search space. The swarm may need several iterations to discover better regions. Premature convergence was the correct diagnosis; the intervention was appropriate, but its effect will only be seen after the swarm has time to explore. | [REVISED_DIAGNOSE] The swarm was completely frozen in a non-global local minimum. The introduction of new, diverse particles is necessary but not instantly sufficient. The best particle remains stuck, meaning the newly introduced diversity must propagate through social attraction before the best can improve. Additional aggressive interventions (e.g., perturbing the best particle) might be needed if no improvement emerges within a few iterations, but for now, patience is advisable.
